defaults.returnSurvivorAliveText:
You report to $PersonPost $personName about the success of your mission, bringing the good news that $sep_sar_survivorFullName was found alive and safely recovered.

Afterward, you order your chief security officer to escort $sep_sar_survivorFullName to a safe location, while you provide a full report detailing the mission and the circumstances of the rescue before receiving your reward.

But before you could continue speaking with $personName, $sep_sar_survivorFullName contacts you.
defaults.survivorDialogText:
"Captain," $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe says, "thank you for saving me. I spoke with my fa... superior, and he wants to give you a greater reward for bringing me back home." 

$sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe pauses for a moment. "Also, if you're hiring, it would be an honor to work with you. I've mostly recovered and convinced my superior to let me go. Now I just need to file a bit of paperwork, so if you ever get the chance, my contact information will be in your directory."

He thanks you once more before ending the connection.
defaults.returnSurvivorDeadText:
You report to $PersonPost $personName about the success of your mission, bringing the grim news that $sep_sar_survivorFullName had already passed away by the time you reached $sep_sar_survivorHimOrHer.

Afterward, you order your chief security officer to escort the body of $sep_sar_survivorFullName to a safe location, while you provide a full report detailing the mission and the circumstances of $sep_sar_survivorFullName's death before receiving your reward.

scenarios.contactMissionBlurb: "A ranking official has filed a search-and-rescue mission. It's a top priority, and they're offering a substantial credit reward."
scenarios.contactMissionOption: Ask about the search and rescue mission
scenarios.contactMissionOfferText:
"$sep_sar_survivorFullName, a close relative of a ranking official, was part of a fleet that was due to return over a week ago, but the fleet never arrived. Fleet intel has marked the fleet as 'lost,' but the official refuses to accept that and insists $sep_sar_survivorLastName is still alive."

$HeOrShe swipes upward on $hisOrHer tripad, sending a shared intel briefing to you. "The fleet's last known heading was toward $sep_sar_possibleLoc, after which all contact was lost."

$HeOrShe leans back slightly. "Your task is to search the area and recover $sep_sar_survivorFullName, alive or dead. If successful, the official is offering $sep_sar_creditReward. Interested?"
scenarios.barMissionBlurb: A gloomy $manOrWoman sits hunched over the bar, clutching a faded photo in $hisOrHer hands. $HisOrHer expression heavy with grief and stubborn hope.
scenarios.barMissionOption: Approach the gloomy $manOrWoman and ask if $heOrShe's alright
scenarios.barMissionOfferText:
$HeOrShe glances at you, suspicious at first, then looks back at the photo with a heavy sigh. "$sep_sar_survivorFullName, my close relative, was part of a fleet that was due to return over a week ago, but the fleet never arrived. Fleet intel has already marked the fleet as 'lost,' but I can't accept that. I know $sep_sar_survivorFirstName is still alive."

$HeOrShe grips the photo and continues. "When I last spoke to $sep_sar_survivorHimOrHer, $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe mentioned going toward $sep_sar_possibleLoc, after which all contact was lost."

$HeOrShe sets the photo down on the table and meets your gaze. "You look like a capable captain, and I don't know who else to turn to. If you're willing to take the risk, I'll pay $sep_sar_creditReward for $sep_sar_survivorFirstName's safe return."
scenarios.entityDialogText:
While searching empty space and drifting wreckage, your sensor officer detects a weak life signature aboard a derelict ship and promptly sends a rescue signal across all frequencies but receives no response.

Without hesitation, you direct your chief salvage operator and chief medical officer to plan and conduct a rescue mission.
scenarios.survivorAliveText: A short time later, your officers find the survivor and recognize him as our rescue target, $sep_sar_survivorFullName. Luckily $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe is alive, and according to your chief medical officer, $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe is comatose and exhibits obvious symptoms of malnutrition, but he will survive.
scenarios.survivorDeadText: A short time later, your officers find the survivor and recognize him as our rescue target, $sep_sar_survivorFullName. Unfortunately, $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe is dead, and according to your chief medical officer, $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe died of extreme malnutrition and oxygen deprivation.

scenarios.contactMissionBlurb: "A ranking official has been kidnapped. I can't spare the resources to mount a rescue, but I trust you enough to offer this mission to you if you're willing."
scenarios.contactMissionOption: Ask about the kidnapped ranking offical
scenarios.contactMissionOfferText:
"$sep_sar_survivorFullName was abducted shortly after leaving a bar. Soon after, the kidnappers contacted $sep_sar_survivorHisOrHer family, demanding a ransom of $sep_sar_creditRansom in exchange for $sep_sar_survivorLastName's release." $heOrShe pauses, transmitting a shared intel briefing from $hisOrHer tripad.

"The kidnappers have been identified as a splinter cell of the $sep_sar_entityFaction, claiming retribution for past actions taken against them. They've ordered the ransom to be delivered to a fleet waiting in $sep_sar_possibleLoc."

$HeOrShe continues, "The family of $sep_sar_survivorLastName is offering a reward of $sep_sar_creditReward for $sep_sar_survivorHisOrHer safe return and the resolution of this matter."
scenarios.barMissionBlurb: You notice an anxious $manOrWoman drinking heavily, muttering complaints about the $Faction on how discounted they treated $himOrHer.
scenarios.barMissionOption: Ask the anxious $manOrWoman on what's bothering $himOrHer
scenarios.barMissionOfferText:
$HeOrShe looks at you, downing another glass before erupting in an outburst. "All I've done for the $Faction... all the years, all the blood... and when my own son is taken, they cannot even spare a single ship to bring $sep_sar_survivorHimOrHer back. That is what I am to them. Nothing. Disposable."

$HeOrShe exhales sharply, forcing $himOrHerself to steady before continuing "$sep_sar_survivorFullName, my son, was abducted while walking home through the district. A group of $sep_sar_entityFaction thugs took him, demanding $sep_sar_creditRansom for his release. They want the ransom delivered to a fleet waiting in $sep_sar_possibleLoc."

His anger fades into weary desperation. "You look like a captain. Someone with a $shipOrFleet, someone who can actually do something. If you help me... if you bring him home... I will see you get $sep_sar_creditReward."
scenarios.entityDialogText:
The comms channel crackles to life, a grainy image of a hard-eyed officer fills the screen. $HisOrHer voice drips with suspicion as $heOrShe sizes up your $shipOrFleet.

"So... you must be the ones carrying the ransom. Hmph. Not much to look at, but I suppose credits don't need much of an escort."

$HeOrShe narrows his eyes, leaning closer to the pickup. "We made it clear. $sep_sar_creditRansom credits, transferred clean and quiet. No tricks, no games. Hand it over, and maybe $sep_sar_survivorFullName walks away instead of ending up as broken flesh."

The channel hangs open, the kidnappers clearly waiting for your next move.
scenarios.entityPayRansomText:
The channel flickers. The hard-eyed officer returns, expression flat. "Payment confirmed. $sep_sar_creditRansom credits received and cleared."

"We are dispatching a single shuttle carrying the hostage. It will intercept your position in approximately fifteen minutes. Keep your fleet at a safe distance. Any attempt to approach or interfere and we will shoot the shuttle down."

The shuttle appears on your sensors. A quick scan returns no weapons or traps and a single life sign aboard. You authorize the shuttle to dock and find $sep_sar_survivorFullName, alive but shaken.
scenarios.entityFightText: The feed crackles back on. The kidnapper sneers, his voice dripping with disappointment. "So that's your choice? I thought you'd have the spine to settle this clean, but I see I was wrong. Fine, if it's a fight you want, then it's a fight you'll get."
scenarios.entityPersuadeToFreeText:
You open comms and begin to persuade the kidnappers. After a tense back-and-forth, they finally relent. Their demands falter as hesitation takes hold, and they choose to release the hostage.

A lone shuttle is launched, carrying $sep_sar_survivorFullName toward your fleet. The hostage is alive but shaken, spared without a single shot fired.
scenarios.entityDefeatedText:
Your sensors officer scans through the debris field, searching for any trace of the hostage. Among the wreckage, they discover a lone shuttle adrift.

Inside, $sep_sar_survivorFullName is found alive, shaken but stable, and quickly transferred to your sickbay for treatment.

scenarios.contactMissionBlurb: "Fleet Intel has filed a search-and-rescue mission for a survey fleet that failed to report back, with orders to recover survivors and secure their data."
scenarios.contactMissionOption: Ask about the missing survey fleet
scenarios.contactMissionOfferText:
"The fleet was sent out on a routine survey mission to chart resources and assess colonization prospects. Their course took them toward $sep_sar_possibleLoc, but all contact was lost en route."

$HeOrShe looks through $hisOrHer tripad and continues, "Fleet intel primarily suspects interference from hostile elements, while others cite navigation failures caused by hyperspace anomalies."

$HeOrShe concludes, "Either way, Fleet intel has authorized a reward of $sep_sar_creditReward to confirm the fleet's fate and recover any surviving crew or data."
scenarios.entityDialogText:
Upon arriving in orbit of $entityName, your sensors officer reports multiple distress signals coming from the surface. Visual sweeps confirm wreckage scattered across a valley, with grounded shuttles nearby and intense thermal signatures still active throughout the area.

You order your comms officer to broadcast emergency hails and stand by for any response.
scenarios.survivorAliveText:
Soon after, multiple responses are received around the wreckage. You immediately begin coordinating a recovery operation to rescue survivors while your comms officer works to determine the cause of the fleet's crash. One transmission is identified as coming from a ranking officer, $sep_sar_survivorFullName.

According to $sep_sar_survivorLastName, the fleet was struck by an unknown hyperspace anomaly that caused its drive bubble to collapse, exposing it to hyperstorms. A few ships managed to reach the system, but damage from the storms rendered them inoperable, forcing the crews to scuttle the remaining vessels for emergency supplies.

You await the completion of the recovery operation and order your crews to accommodate the arriving survivors.
scenarios.survivorDeadText:
After a period of waiting, no responses are received from the surface. With no other option, you dispatch medical and security teams to inspect the grounded emergency shuttles.

The teams report back with grim findings. The shuttles are filled with bodies, most malnourished or dead from oxygen depletion after their life support failed. No survivors remain.

Further investigation reveals that the fleet was struck by an unknown hyperspace anomaly that caused its drive bubble to collapse, exposing it to hyperstorms. A few ships managed to reach the system, but damage from the storms rendered them inoperable, forcing the crews to scuttle the remaining vessels for emergency supplies.

You order the recovery of the remains to ensure they are properly accounted for and given due respect.
scenarios.returnSurvivorAliveText:
You report to $PersonPost $personName about the success of your mission, bringing the good news that a few $sep_sar_subjectName were found alive and safely recovered.

Afterward, you order your chief security officer to escort the survivors to a safe location while you provide a full report detailing the mission and the circumstances of the rescue before receiving your reward.

Before you can continue speaking with $personName, $sep_sar_survivorFullName contacts you.
scenarios.survivorDialogText:
"Captain," $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe says, "On behalf of the fleet, thank you for saving us. We spoke with our superiors, and we requested to give you a greater reward for bringing us home."

$sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe pauses for a moment. "Some of the crew wish to join you and so do I. I've mostly recovered and convinced my superior to let me go. I just need to file some paperwork, so if you ever get the chance, the crew are available for hire while my contact information will be in your directory."

He thanks you once more before ending the connection.
scenarios.returnSurvivorDeadText:
You report to $PersonPost $personName about the success of your mission, bringing the grim news that no survivors remained by the time you arrived.

Afterward, you order your chief security officer to escort the recovered remains to a secure location while you provide a full report detailing the mission and the circumstances of the fleet's destruction before receiving your reward.

scenarios.contactMissionBlurb: A patrol fleet was ambushed after discovering a pirate hideout. Survivors were taken hostage, and a mission has been filed to search and rescue them.
scenarios.contactMissionOption: Ask about rescuing the captured patrol crews
scenarios.contactMissionOfferText:
$HeOrShe interacts with $hisOrHer tripad, sending you a brief mission intel. "A routine patrol operating in $sep_sar_possibleLoc didn't return as scheduled. Their fate only became known when the pirates contacted us, demanding a ransom of $sep_sar_creditRansom for the surviving crew they had taken hostage."

$HeOrShe continues, "Fleet Intelligence determined that the hostages are being held in a pirate hideout. From intercepted communications, they've assessed the strength of the site and estimate that at least $sep_sar_marineAmount marines will be required to free the captives."

$HeOrShe adds, "In return for rescuing the patrol crew, a reward of $sep_sar_creditReward will be provided."
scenarios.entityDialogText:
Upon arriving in orbit of the planet, your sensors officer initiates a surface scan. The sweep passes over mountain ranges and canyons until faint energy signatures and concealed structures are detected deep within the terrain. The readings confirm the presence of the pirate hideout.

With the location identified, your officers and crew await your decision on how to proceed.
scenarios.entityPayRansomText:
You agree to the kidnappers' terms and transfer the ransom as instructed. Soon after, a lone shuttle departs the planet and approaches your ship.

Your sensors officer scans the shuttle, confirming multiple life signs aboard and no possible traps. Once docked, the hatch opens and the weary hostages emerge. $sep_sar_survivorFullName, a ranking official, steps forward and thanks you on behalf of the survivors before being escorted to medical care.
scenarios.entityPersuadeToFreeText:
You persuade the kidnappers to release the hostages without payment. After a tense exchange, they agree and send a lone shuttle toward your ship.

Your sensors officer scans the shuttle, confirming multiple life signs aboard and no possible traps. Once docked, the hatch opens and the weary hostages emerge. $sep_sar_survivorFullName, a ranking official, steps forward and thanks you on behalf of the survivors before being escorted to medical care.
scenarios.entityRaidFinishedText:
Your marines advance into the pirate hideout with practiced precision, clearing the compound room by room. A few hostages are lost in the crossfire, but the assault is controlled and decisive, preventing further casualties.

During the operation, $sep_sar_survivorFullName, a ranking official, steps forward and directs your raid leader to the remaining survivors. They are secured and extracted back to your fleet for treatment and safety.
scenarios.returnSurvivorAliveText:
You report to $PersonPost $personName about the success of your mission, bringing the good news that a few $sep_sar_subjectName were found alive and safely recovered.

Afterward, you order your chief security officer to escort the survivors to a safe location while you provide a full report detailing the mission and the circumstances of the rescue before receiving your reward.

Before you can continue speaking with $personName, $sep_sar_survivorFullName contacts you.
scenarios.survivorDialogText:
"Captain," $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe says, "On behalf of the fleet, thank you for saving us. We spoke with our superiors, and we requested to give you a greater reward for bringing us home."

$sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe pauses for a moment. "Some of the crew wish to join you and so do I. I've mostly recovered and convinced my superior to let me go. I just need to file some paperwork, so if you ever get the chance, the crew are available for hire while my contact information will be in your directory."

He thanks you once more before ending the connection.
scenarios.returnSurvivorDeadText:
You report to $PersonPost $personName about the success of your mission, bringing the grim news that no survivors remained by the time you arrived.

Afterward, you order your chief security officer to escort the recovered remains to a secure location while you provide a full report detailing the mission and the circumstances of rescue before receiving your reward.
